heading: Powers
label: 21303 Powers
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)2130336:20 (2d sentence)., (2d sentence), ; , , .Jan. 4, 1889, ch. 2025 Stat. 640Pub. L. 85–83, § 171 Stat. 276July 3, 1957In clause (1), the words “not inconsistent with law” are omitted as unnecessary.In clause (2), the word “property” is substituted for “real and personal estate”, and the word “necessary” is substituted for “so far only as may be necessary”, to eliminate unnecessary words. The words “to carry out the purposes of the corporation” are substituted for “to its lawful ends” for consistency in the revised title.

Tax Exemption of Real Property, , , provided that:Pub. L. 85–83, § 271 Stat. 277July 3, 1957“The real property situated in Square 817, in the city of Washington, District of Columbia, described as lot 23, owned, occupied, and used by the American Historical Association, is exempt from all taxation so long as the same is so owned and occupied, and not used for commercial purposes, subject to the provisions of sections 2, 3, and 5 of the Act entitled ‘An Act to define the real property exempt from taxation in the District of Columbia’, approved (; D.C. Code, secs. 47–801b, 47–801c, and 47–801e).”December 24, 194256 Stat. 1089

children.text: adopt a constitution and bylaws; and
hold property in the District of Columbia necessary to carry out the purposes of the corporation.
